example_prompt: Create a Safaricom premium SMS subscription for a Kenyan phone number using Africa's Talking.

shortCode.description: Valid shortcode registered with Africa's Talking for Safaricom.

requestId.description: Client-specified identifier echoed in the delivery report (DLR) callback.

redirectUrl.description: URL the user is redirected to after confirming the subscription.

phoneNumber.description: Phone number to subscribe in E.164 format (e.g. +254711082282).

sourceIP.description: IP address of the subscribing party.

userAgent.description: Browser user agent string of the subscribing party.

responseCode.description: Result of the request. 'Success' indicates the subscription was initiated.

status.description: Subscription status. Typically 'Sent' when the request is accepted.

transactionId.description: Unique identifier for the subscription transaction.

url.description: Redirect URL returned only in HE (Header Enrichment) mode. Absent in Subscribe Manage mode.

responseCode.description: Non-Success response code indicating why the request failed.

gotchas: This endpoint is Safaricom-specific (Kenya only). The URL path includes /safaricom — there is no generic subscription endpoint across all carriers.
Production uses content.africastalking.com, NOT api.africastalking.com. Sandbox uses api.sandbox.africastalking.com. The domain difference is a common integration mistake.

Two subscription modes exist: HE (Header Enrichment) requires the user to be on Safaricom mobile data and returns a redirect url; Subscribe Manage sends a USSD prompt. The mode is determined by Safaricom's routing — you cannot choose it explicitly. Only HE responses include the url field.
